paragraphs.body: I started with the manual assignment where I had to compare and contrast the two places. I was confused at the beginning about how to turn a comparison-based essay into a story that can be expressed in the form of animation. Finally, we decided to make 2 characters traveling to different locations and introduce their hometown. This way we can animate the essay into a story. Since the video is only around 1 minute long, we don’t have tons of work to do. One disadvantage is that Vyond is not allowing people to collaborate on different devices. I have to edit every scene on my laptop while the other students can’t help much even though they are willing to do so. The good thing is that making an animation like this is not as complicated as I thought. There are enough characters and props for me to choose. There were even edited clips, such as our airplane clip, that we could use without editing. This made my work easier and saved a lot of my time. Our second assignment will be AI-generated. The options were very limited when it comes to choosing the background. Our topic is bridges, but we couldn’t find anything related to that. As a result, we picked a random scene that includes dialogue. The following is the prompt we used to generate the video: Generate a 1-min animated video to tell the story of two bridges—Smithfield Street Bridge in Pittsburgh and Yangmingtan Bridge in Harbin—in the next 50 years. Both bridges will face the challenges of a rapidly changing world, and technology will play a critical role in their evolution. In the first scene, focus on how autonomous electric vehicles will travel seamlessly across the Smithfield Street Bridge, while on the Yangmingtan Bridge, AI-driven systems will monitor traffic and enhance safety for pedestrians and cyclists. Local businesses near both bridges will utilize AI to offer personalized services and products to visitors. In the second scene, highlight the use of sustainable energy and innovative designs in response to climate change. The Smithfield Street Bridge will feature solar panels and green spaces that manage stormwater, while the Yangmingtan Bridge will incorporate snow-melting technology and wind turbines to harness renewable energy. In the final scene, synthesize the key changes that will occur over the next 50 years, showcasing both bridges as symbols of connectivity and resilience, celebrating their roles as vital links in their communities while embracing the future. This prompt was written by Victoria, one of our group members. Apparently, he put a lot of effort into the prompt with detailed illustrations of each scene. However, the video generated was not very ideal. For example, the dialogue included many words that should not be spoken such as “in the final scene.” We have to get rid of that part so the dialogue sounds smooth. Since we are on the free trial, we can only generate the video 3 times a day. That’s even more challenging because our chances are limited. On a scale of 1 to 10, I give the AI-generated video a 7. The video was around 1 minute and it’s overall expressing our ideas. However, I prefer the self-edited video because I put more effort into that one.

What I learned from this experience is that we should not rely on AI too much, at least not for now. AI still has a long way to go and improvement will be needed. At this point, I believe we are more capable of creating our own masterpieces than AI. They are cool in some way such as brainstorming and planning, but when it comes to editing, our brain is the best tool to produce and convey our ideas.
